alias_policy.summary: Serve every surfaced compatibility route at both the root path and a /v1-prefixed alias. Treat root as the canonical path for native/helper clients and /v1 as the canonical alias for web clients.

classification_policy.required: Explicitly specified in COMPAT_SPEC_V0.md or needed to preserve the documented helper/web core contract with stable payload expectations.
classification_policy.stub-required: Surfaced by reverse/web evidence but missing strong payload/status guarantees; implement as permissive compatibility stubs first.
classification_policy.out-of-scope: Surfaced upstream but excluded from this self-hosted v1 scope, especially billing, admin, catalog, roadmap, forge, manager, and similar non-core features.

open_questions: Reverse notes infer POST /rom/lookup and POST /conflicts/check, while COMPAT_SPEC_V0.md canonizes GET variants. Freeze GET as canonical and keep POST as optional compatibility stubs if runtime capture later confirms them.
The matrix assumes the root path and /v1 alias should both exist for every surfaced route, even when only one form is currently observed in a specific client.
